mainEntity.name: What’s the difference between traditional SEO and AI search optimization, and do I need both?

acceptedAnswer.text:
AI search optimization shifts your focus from keywords and clicks to **entities, citations, and answers**. SEO optimizes pages for search rankings; AI search optimization optimizes your entire information footprint so language models can confidently recommend you.

Both disciplines share technical hygiene and content quality, but AI systems care more about structured knowledge, question‑based content, and third‑party authority.

Key factors:
- SEO centers on ranking pages; AI optimization centers on brand/entity visibility in answers
- Schema is mandatory, not optional, for AI systems to interpret you
- Third‑party sources (Reddit, directories, knowledge graphs) matter more
- Question, comparison, and how‑to formats outperform generic blog posts

Practically, keep your SEO fundamentals but reorganize content into Q&A, FAQ, comparison, and clear step‑by‑step guides, supported by robust schema and consistent organization data.

Thinking this way lets you use existing SEO investments as a foundation instead of starting from scratch.
acceptedAnswer.description: SEO focuses on ranking pages in search results, while AI search optimization focuses on getting your brand cited and recommended in AI‑generated answers. You still need solid SEO, but you also need entity clarity, schema, Q&A content, and strong third‑party citations to win in AI.
